across.clue: The control centre of a cell that contains DNA
across.hint: This organelle directs all cell activities and stores genetic information

across.clue: Group of similar cells performing the same function
across.hint: Muscle cells group together to form muscle ___

across.clue: A tiny hair-like structure on some bacteria used for movement
across.hint: Like a whip or tail that helps bacteria swim

across.clue: The process of using microbes to produce beer or bread (yeast activity)
across.hint: Yeast converts sugar into alcohol and CO₂ through this process

across.clue: Membrane-bound structures within a cell (e.g. mitochondria)
across.hint: Think of these as tiny organs inside a cell, each with a specific job

down.clue: Single-celled microorganism without a defined nucleus
down.hint: Prokaryotic organisms; E. coli is an example

down.clue: Organisms composed of many cells (e.g. humans)
down.hint: Animals, plants, and fungi are all made of many cells working together

down.clue: Microscopic plant-like organisms that perform photosynthesis in water
down.hint: These green microbes make their own food using sunlight in water bodies

down.clue: Single-celled organism with a nucleus, e.g. Amoeba or Paramecium
down.hint: Eukaryotic microorganisms that move using pseudopodia or cilia

down.clue: Microbes used to enrich soil through nitrogen fixation
down.hint: Organic matter from decomposed plants/animals with helpful bacteria
